---
title: TypeScript coding rules
scope: project
---

<!-- Generated by `harness rules bootstrap`. Edit to tune; re-run to regenerate. -->

- NEVER use `any`; use `unknown` for genuinely-unknown values and narrow via `typeof`/`in`/discriminated unions before use. Don't silence `no-unsafe-*` errors by casting — fix the type. Use `as` only for assertions you can justify, never `as any`.
- MUST handle every Promise: `await` it, `return` it, or `void` it. Never leave a floating promise (`no-floating-promises`), and never pass an `async` function where a `void`-returning callback is expected — e.g. React event handlers, `Array.forEach` (`no-misused-promises`).
- Prefer `??` over `||` for defaulting (so `0`/`""`/`false` survive) and optional chaining `a?.b?.c` over `&&` chains. Don't write conditions the types prove always-truthy/falsy (`no-unnecessary-condition`).
- Use `import type { … }` / `export type { … }` for type-only imports so they're erased from the bundle and don't trigger side-effects (`consistent-type-imports`); critical for Next.js `'use server'`/server-only modules.
- Prefer `satisfies` to validate an object against a type while keeping its narrow inferred literal types; use explicit annotations only when you need widening. Don't annotate what TS already infers correctly.
- Type function inputs and exported/public return types explicitly; let local returns infer. Use `void` (not `any`) for ignored callback returns, and keep callback params required.
- Model variant data as discriminated unions (shared literal `kind`/`type` field) and switch on it; add a `never`-typed `default` for exhaustiveness so new variants fail compilation.
- Use `unknown` in `catch` (don't assume `Error`); narrow before accessing `.message`. Prefer typed error/result envelopes over throwing across LangGraph node and API-route boundaries.
- NEVER use `@ts-ignore`; if unavoidable use `@ts-expect-error` with a one-line reason so it errors once the underlying issue is fixed.
- Prefer `readonly` arrays/props and `as const` for literal config/tuples; avoid mutating shared state. Use `Record<K, V>`, `Pick`, `Omit`, `Parameters`, `ReturnType` instead of hand-duplicating shapes.
- In React/Next.js: type props with explicit `interface`/`type` (no `React.FC` for children-implicit typing); type `useState`/`useRef` generics when inference is `undefined`/`null`; keep server-only types and secrets out of Client Component (`'use client'`) modules.
- Use lowercase primitives (`string`, `number`, `boolean`, `object`), never the boxed `String`/`Number`/`Object`. Avoid non-null `!` assertions except where invariants are truly guaranteed.
